publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-01-21 18:19:04 -0500.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Photographs, illustrations from books and magazines, and a few printed articles.
did.abstract: The Hartmann Collection's main focus is Vietnam, but there is material on Cambodia and Laos, as well as a few items on Burma and Thailand. There are numerous scenes of all parts of Indochina, with particular attention to various important architectural sites. Historical materials emphasize the colonial period. Of particular interest are an extensive collection of portraits of Vietnamese and Cambodian monarchs from the colonial period and a wide and (generally) well-labelled selection of pictures showing the many different ethnic groups in Vietnam.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The Hartmann Collection is a compilation of photographs, illustrations from books and magazines, and a few printed articles. The main focus of the collection is Vietnam, but there is material on Cambodia and Laos, as well as a few items on Burma and Thailand. There are numerous scenes of all parts of Indochina, with particular attention to various important architectural sites (in Hue and Angkor, for example) and to the Saigon area. Included are pictures of various stores and factories in South Vietnam during the post-1954 period. Historical materials emphasize the colonial period; there are a number of illustrations taken from books and periodicals about the colonization of Vietnam and Cambodia by the French in the 19th century. Numerous photographs of individuals can be found; of particular interest are an extensive collection of portraits of Vietnamese and Cambodian monarchs from the colonial period and a wide and (generally) well-labelled selection of pictures showing the many different ethnic groups in Vietnam. There are a considerable number of maps, especially numerous are road maps, including some produced by the Vietnamese government, and street/tourist guides to Saigon. Also, catalogs of Hartmann's collections.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Hartmann collection on Indochina, #4298, Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Vietnam, Colonial period - Prominent Vietnamese (not officials)
Vietnam, Colonial period - Prominent Frenchmen (not officials)
Vietnam, Colonial period - Views - Tonkin (excluding Hanoi)
Vietnam, Colonial period - Views - Cochin China (excluding Saigon)
Vietnam, Colonial period - Education and health
Cambodia, Colonial period - Monarchs and officials
Vietnam, Tenth to fifteenth centuries (Ngo, Ly, Tran Dynasties)
Vietnam, Fifteenth to eighteenth centuries (Le Dynasty)
Vietnam, Eighteenth to Nineteenth centuries (Tayso'n and Nguyen Dynasties to the French invasion)
Vietnam, First and second Indochina wars
Vietnam, Cities - Saigon - Street maps and guides
Vietnam, Southern - Saigon - Government buildings and monuments
Veitnam, Crops - Tea, coffee, areca, betel, palm, tobacco
Vietnam, Crops - Peanuts, pepper, soybeans, sugar cane
Vietnam, Service industries - hotels, restaurants, entertainment
Origional Catalogue of the Harmann collection (in French); French Cultural Mission to South Vietnam high school syllabi, 1967; Five family photographs
